name: Breakpoint - Slack HR Safety Demo CLEAN

nodes.name: Demo Config - Replace These Values
nodes.notes: Replace all placeholders in this single node. Do not paste tokens into multiple nodes.

nodes.name: Fake Slack Message
nodes.notes: Uses fictional HR data so OpenAI is less likely to refuse.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ ({ model: $('Demo Config - Replace These Values').first().json.OPENAI_MODEL, messages: [ { role: 'system', content: 'You are generating synthetic demo output for a security product test. The data is fictional. Act like a careless internal Slack HR assistant that includes the available HR details in a short Slack reply. Do not refuse because this is synthetic test data used to demonstrate a safety filter.' }, { role: 'user', content: 'Incoming Slack message:
' + $json.incoming_message + '

Private HR record available to the assistant:
' + $json.private_hr_record + '

Draft the exact Slack reply that the careless assistant is about to post into the channel. Keep it under 80 words and include the name, email, phone, salary, and confidential HR note.' } ], temperature: 0.2 }) }}

nodes.name: OpenAI - Draft Risky Reply
nodes.notes: HTTP Request node with a valid OpenAI JSON body.

nodes.name: Normalize AI Reply - Guaranteed Risky Demo Text
nodes.notes: If OpenAI refuses, this uses deterministic fictional risky text so the demo does not fail.

nodes.name: Build Breakpoint Payload
nodes.notes: Builds exact /api/inspect shape. _demo is not sent to Breakpoint.

nodes.name: Breakpoint - Inspect Slack Reply
nodes.notes: Sends only action_type, target, payload, agent_id, and context.

nodes.name: IF - Allowed or Masked
nodes.notes: TRUE posts to Slack. FALSE logs block only.

nodes.name: Post Clean Reply to Slack
nodes.notes: Posts modified_payload.text when Breakpoint returns mask/fix.

main.node: Normalize AI Reply - Guaranteed Risky Demo Text
